free_text: Proton induced resonance reaction on K-41
(I). A high-resolution investigation of the
K-41(p,alpha0)Ar-38 reaction.
The Utrecht 3 MV Van de Graaff.
A 41K(2)SO(4), enriched in K-41 to 99.2%, evaporated in
vacuo onto a 10 mug/cm**2 carbon backing, was used as
target.
seven Si detectors 50 mm**2 active area
*By authors*. For the energy calibration the following
resonances have been used- Al-27(p,alpha)Mg-24 at
Ni-58(p,gamma)Cu-59 at E(p)=1843.7+-0.9 keV.
The yield of the 1559 keV resonance has been measured
in an absolute way with a thick target and relative to
the known yield of the E(p)=1637 keV resonance in
Cl-35(p,alpa)S-32. Two values agree within the combined
experimental error of 30%. The average was used as a
standard. The strength of the other resonances has been
calculated by comparing the area under the resonance
peaks with the area of the 1559 keV resonance.
Excitation energy of 42Ca.
Error in excitation energy of 42Ca
